summary: W3-B 焕然一新 — restyle the Fabric CLI from flat coloured lines to a structural visual language (section-bar ▌ + tree ├─└ + grid padEnd + badge [ok]/[team]). Two-layer primitive split per C-003: parity-trivial primitives (section-bar, badge, indent) land in the shared theme.ts + its byte-mirror theme.cjs (parity stays green); complex primitives (tree/grid) land in a new CLI-only tui/structure.ts with NO cjs mirror. Four reskinned surfaces (doctor / install ConsoleOutputRenderer / error / HUD) each ship a NO_COLOR vitest output snapshot (C-008 per-surface increment). picocolors is deleted (already a dead dep). 7-token palette VALUES and @clack prompt controls are untouched.
approach: Foundation-first DAG. Wave 1 builds the structural-primitive vocabulary (F-001) and removes the dead picocolors dep (F-007) — both have no upstream deps. Wave 2 fans out the consumers in parallel: the @clack wrap API (F-002, install context only) and the four per-surface reskins (F-003 doctor, F-004 install renderer, F-006 error, F-005 HUD). Each per-surface task extracts/uses a pure renderX(data):string function and bundles its own NO_COLOR snapshot test (C-008 — snapshots are NOT a separate phase). F-005 is the only consumer that touches the shared layer (it renders via the .cjs hook), so any shared primitive it needs must be byte-mirrored into theme.cjs and keep theme-parity green. Wave 3 is a single goal-backward verification gate (tsc + knip + full test suite + theme-parity + all NO_COLOR snapshots + grep no picocolors). Incremental per-surface commits on branch feat/w3b-vivid-reskin; no big-bang.

stages: build structural primitives (theme.ts parity-trivial: section-bar/badge-scope; tui/structure.ts CLI-only: tree/grid + ASCII fallback)
mirror parity-trivial additions into theme.cjs; keep theme-parity byte-identical
wrap @clack context (themeIntro/outro/log/note) without touching prompt controls
reskin each surface (doctor / install / error / HUD) via pure renderX(data) primitives
snapshot NO_COLOR output of each surface (producer-consumer round-trip oracle)
final gate: tsc + knip + full tests + theme-parity + all snapshots + grep no picocolors

design_decisions: colors.ts FATE = Option A (KEEP). Rationale: colors.ts is already a thin theme adapter (ux-w2-5) re-exporting paint/symbol/isColorEnabled + CLI-only width helpers (displayWidth/padEnd needing string-width). Deleting it (Option B) would churn ~14 import sites for no behavioural gain and violates 'minimize changes'. New CLI-only structural primitives (tree/grid) go in a NEW sibling module tui/structure.ts so colors.ts stays a stable adapter; structure.ts imports padEnd/displayWidth/isColorEnabled from colors.ts.

Two-layer primitive split (C-003): parity-trivial primitives (section-bar ▌, badge [ok]/[team], indent) are added to the SHARED theme.ts AND byte-mirrored into theme.cjs because the HUD (.cjs hook) needs them and theme-parity must stay green. COMPLEX primitives (tree ├─└│, grid padEnd columns + rule line) live ONLY in tui/structure.ts (TS surfaces: doctor/install/error) and are NEVER mirrored — keeping the parity surface small.

HUD (F-005) stays parity-safe: it renders via badge + section-bar + plain indent only (the parity-trivial shared primitives), NOT the complex CLI-only tree. Mockup #3's ├─ tree in the HUD is rendered with a MINIMAL inline indent/branch helper that is byte-mirrored into theme.cjs IF used; default is plain two-space indent to avoid growing the parity surface.

snapshots are per-surface (C-008), NOT a separate F-008 phase. Each reskin task (F-003/004/005/006) bundles its own NEW vitest toMatchSnapshot test with NO_COLOR=1. F-008 collapses into the final verify task (TASK-007) as the green-gate over all snapshots.

@clack scope (C-006): only intro/outro/log/note context is wrapped (new install/theme-clack.ts). prompt controls text()/select()/confirm()/multiselect() call sites are NOT touched — grep-verified unchanged.
picocolors (F-007) is folded into the foundation wave as its own tiny task (TASK-002) — it is a one-line package.json delete + one stale comment edit, with zero real imports already verified.
ASCII fallback is gated by isColorEnabled() inside each primitive (├─→+-, │→|, └─→`- , ▌→#, ━/─→= /-, badges→plain [ok]); NO_COLOR snapshots pin the ASCII branch.

patterns: pure renderX(data):string functions + establish a NEW vitest toMatchSnapshot test with NO_COLOR=1 (grouped-help.test.ts uses toContain/toMatch, not toMatchSnapshot, and does not pin NO_COLOR — so each surface adds its own NO_COLOR=1 toMatchSnapshot test)
theme.ts ↔ theme.cjs byte-parity census (theme-parity.test.ts) for any shared-layer addition
semantic role tokens via paint(token,text,colorOn) — never literal colour names
isColorEnabled()-gated ASCII fallback for every structural glyph
conventions: ESM imports in src/ (.js extensions), CommonJS require in templates/hooks/*.cjs
strict TypeScript; pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit must be clean
incremental per-surface git commits on feat/w3b-vivid-reskin
dependencies: string-width (via colors.ts displayWidth/padEnd — wide-char safe grid)
